hypothesis: eBPF Bytecode Modification:** Inject an `BPF_ALU64` instruction sequence at the start of the packet/data processing pipeline to compute a lightweight heuristic of data entropy (e.g., counting adjacent out-of-order elements in the first 64 bytes). Based on this volatility score, modify the `BPF_JMP_TAIL_CALL` instruction to dynamically route execution. High-entropy chunks will branch to an eBPF program optimized for branchless Quicksort partitioning, while low-entropy chunks will tail-call into a SIMD-accelerated bitonic merge routine.
